stackRefinements.core: Node ESM library; zod schema/validation — likely the shared domain/types layer
stackRefinements.svc: Fastify HTTP service, TypeScript-compiled (tsc build), node:test for tests
stackRefinements.web: React 18 SPA built with Vite, ESM
stackRefinements.test-kit: Shared test utilities built on vitest (vitest is a runtime dependency of the package, but the package itself is consumed dev-only)
stackRefinements.legacy: No manifest; contains only src/index.js — plain JavaScript, stack unconfirmed

ordering.notes: core is the sole runtime provider (consumed by svc via workspace:* and by web via file: link) — it changes first when a task spans layers. test-kit is a provider only through a dev-only edge into svc, so it carries a weaker ordering signal. svc and web have no edge between them and can change in parallel after core. legacy participates in no edges; its position in the order is arbitrary.

gaps: legacy layer (packages/legacy) has no recognizable manifest (no package.json) — internal edges and external deps for it cannot be determined; only src/index.js exists
web consumes core via a relative file:../core link instead of the pnpm workspace:* protocol used elsewhere — same edge semantically, but inconsistent linking worth confirming with the team
test-kit lists vitest under dependencies (not devDependencies) while every package's test script uses node --test — test-runner intent is ambiguous
